title: navarretia viscidula subsp. purpurea
intro: Navarretia viscidula subsp. purpurea, also known as Purple Marsh St. John's-crowfoot, is a plant species belonging to the Phrymaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Navarretia viscidula subsp. purpurea include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
